sentences.text: Hi, Michelle.
Hi, how are you?
I am good.
I am.
So, wherever you are it looks like summertime.
It is bright outside.
This is so we have like a four season room, like off our kitchen.
So you have like the brick over here and then like, it's all glass.
I love it.
It's so great.
It is.
Yeah.
And it was a couple of years ago when I started working from home a couple days a week.
I was like, this is where I want my death because I refuse to, like, live in a box.
So I love that.
Oh, my goodness.
It's so nice.
I am apologizing ahead of time.
It looks like I've been crying.
I have to preface by saying I had pink eye.
I got it over the weekend.
I woke up Saturday morning.
Mind you, I have two kids and they don't have it.
Where, where I got it is a mystery by the way.
So at least they're healthy.
But that's why I look rough right now.
You're right.
I've had the same thing happen.
I'm like, get your fingers.
I drive.
I mean, my, my three year old he sneezed in my face on Thursday.
And I'm like, ok, that was disgusting.
I thought nothing of it though.
But then I woke up to, like, between three and 4 a.m. and I'm like, no.
Oh, that's terrible.
Yes.
Yes.
But it's ok.
We're good.
I just want to throw that out there.
That, yes, I look like I've been crying but I'm good.
Well, you look wonderful.
I would not have guessed.
Well, thank you, Michelle.
Um You'll see.
I have Justin on the call.
He's not going to go on camera.
Um He is out in Scottsdale.
Um but he is our product expert.
So he's gonna go through the platform before we get into things.
Um Just in my mind, I thought we could talk about what your current process is for gathering comp data, your market survey process and just get better understanding of your overall business goals.
Justin's gonna go through the um the apartment IQ platform and he can showcase what that might look like integrated to your current process.
And then at the end, we can decide whether it makes sense to continue the conversation or at this time, we wanna revisit down the line.
So does that work?
Yeah.
So right now a lot of our comp data,
I mean, I guess there's two fold.

So sites do call out to their comps weekly um and ask just about traffic and occupancy, but you can never trust what a comp is telling you Um And so we use Real Page um right now, so say in a capacity, I mean, I use it just to figure out like if we have a proper under performing, I'm looking to see how the market's doing or if we're um overall, our company goal is to double in size in the next four years, we've secured the funding for that and now it's just finding the properties to acquire or to build.

So getting to know we're going into a lot of new markets and that's one reason I want to make sure that we have the best software possible um because we're looking at expanding and it's so important to really have that grasp on those new markets that we aren't as familiar with.
Um So right now I rely on real page to really give me that information.
OK.
And do you feel like as of right now it's hitting the mark on everything or do you feel like it's missing certain pieces that you were hoping for?
Yeah.
So I'm really comfortable with Yardie Matrix because I used it for forever um for pretty much my entire career.
And then in September last year, I actually um started in with Ackerman and they use Real Page.
So I'm still getting to know the platform itself.
Um It's not, for me, it's not as user friendly as Yardie Matrix.
But again, I'm like, I have to give him a break because I just so familiar with yardi.
Um, but I mean, I think for now it's like accomplishing the information.
Um, it's, or it's giving me the information that I need.
So.
Ok.
And then in regards to you said you're, the company's goal is to double in size and that's through, is that through development or acquisitions or combination, a combination of both?
Ok.
Um And how are you for acquisition purposes?
How are you guys identifying properties that you guys want to look into?
So, acquisitions has a whole formula for this.
Um And so they're looking at markets we want, we're trying to target um either just um particularly distressed loans that they, they're looking at like loans that are coming due and things like that.
Um But we wanna look at properties right in like a B um we're trying to get out of the C class overall.
Um And then we wanna be able to either add value.
Um so do a renovation or if it's a brand new,
I, I assume they co star for that or are they using a different tool they're used as far as I know real page is the only tool we have.
Ok.

Yeah, so that's why like, I just don't know, I would love to have Costar because I just know how encompassing it is, but I've never, I mean, I've never used it but like the um you know, you get apartments.com will just send you the data and you see this big report and it looks so pretty and it's like, oh my gosh, I need this all the time.
I need this at my fingertips and then you get a price and it's like, well, who can afford that?
Actually, no one can afford that.
What are you thinking?
Exactly.
Yes.
Yes.
So, I, I mean, again, I'm trying, I'm still trying to get used to Real Page.
Um
It's not, I just, I'm not a huge fan of the platform.
